hypothesis: Incorporating uncertainty-aware concentration estimation as an auxiliary output head—trained to predict a probability distribution over possible mixture ratios rather than accepting a fixed scalar concentration as input—will enable the transformer model to compensate for the imprecise LC-MS concentration measurements that currently degrade performance at low target-compound fractions, thereby improving Top-10 accuracy for minority components (≤20% concentration) by at least 10 percentage points compared to the fixed-concentration conditioning approach demonstrated in this paper.
